slides.title: PlugForge
slides.subtitle: Turning an app into a platform with API-as-contract discipline
slides.thesis: The grade is not the number of endpoints. It is whether a stranger reaches a verified signed webhook in under 30 minutes — from docs alone.
slides.footer: Monday 1:00 PM CT · One-week sprint · Epics 1–7

slides.title: Stop being an app. Start being a platform.
slides.subtitle: Stripe, GitHub, Slack, Shopify — each got dramatically more valuable the day a third party could build on top.

cards.title: Versioned public API
cards.body: /api/v1/ — consistent error shape, cursor pagination, generated OpenAPI

cards.title: OAuth 2.0 done right
cards.body: Auth Code + PKCE for web, Device Grant for CLI, granular scopes

cards.title: Signed webhooks
cards.body: HMAC-SHA256 + timestamp, retries w/ backoff, DLQ, replay

cards.title: A typed SDK
cards.body: @ship/sdk — parity-tested against the spec, drift fails CI

callout.title: What the grade measures
callout.body: Depth over breadth. Proof over promises. A small public API that matches its spec beats a sprawling one that contradicts it.

slides.title: The platform boundary
slides.subtitle: Auth, scope, rate-limit, audit and webhook publication attach only at the public layer.
slides.caption: High-level diagram — rendered from the mermaid block in ArchDefense.md

slides.title: How a request reaches a domain service
slides.subtitle: Internal routes call the same domain services directly — the public layer adds the platform concerns.

panels.title: PUBLIC  /api/v1/*
bullets: Fresh router. Owns AuthN, AuthZ, rate-limit, audit, webhooks.
A lint rule fails the build if it imports an internal handler.
Every failure ships ApiError { code, message, request_id }.

panels.title: INTERNAL  /api/*
bullets: First-party UI traffic; no public contract obligations.
Calls the SAME domain services — single source of truth.
The public/internal split is a one-way door. Enforced, not hoped.

slides.title: Two flows, chosen for two clients
slides.subtitle: RFC 6749 + 7636 (PKCE) + 8628 (Device Grant), hand-rolled for correctness.

columns.heading: Auth Code + PKCE
steps: User consents on Ship's own screen
columns.why: PKCE binds the code to the client without shipping a secret to the browser. The negative case is mandatory.

columns.heading: Device Grant
steps: User verifies code in any browser
columns.why: A CLI can't safely hold a secret or catch a redirect. Device flow fits the headless client exactly.

slides.title: Time-to-First-Event: the rubric
slides.subtitle: A platform is judged by how fast a stranger composes a useful loop on top of it.
slides.terminal:
$ pnpm install @ship/sdk
$ ship login            # device flow
$ ship docs create --title "hello"
$ ship webhooks tail    # stream deliveries
→ document.created  signature verified ✓
slides.callout: pnpm drill ttfe runs install → login → subscribe → create → receive → verify against a containerized Ship — and catches contract regressions faster than any unit test.

slides.title: Priority order: contract before everything
slides.subtitle: OAuth and the public/internal boundary go in on Day 1 — they are one-way doors.

items.title: OAuth foundation
items.body: Auth Code + PKCE end-to-end, negative tests included. Device Grant same day.

items.title: API boundary
items.body: Fresh /api/v1 router, lint rule, error fitness test = the TODO list.

items.title: OpenAPI gen
items.body: Generator working on documents before adding issues, sprints, me.

items.title: Webhooks e2e
items.body: Registry → bus → subs → signer → deliverer → log → replay, one day.

items.title: SDK + CLI
items.body: CLI consumes the SDK as it's built; worst bugs surface against a real consumer.

items.title: TTFE drill
items.body: From Day 5 — once SDK + one resource exist, the drill exists.

items.title: Agent rewire
items.body: Portal eats the dog food. Agent becomes a citizen behind a feature flag.

slides.title: What I'm committing to ship

rows.title: OAuth that passes
rows.body: Auth Code + PKCE end-to-end, wrong verifier → invalid_grant. Device Grant honors slow_down.

rows.title: A contract
rows.body: Every /api/v1 route: scoped, ApiError-shaped, in the generated OpenAPI, cursor-paginated if a list.

rows.title: Webhooks you trust
rows.body: HMAC + timestamp, backoff + DLQ + replay, verified by the SDK in one line.

rows.title: A CLI that proves it
rows.body: ship login / docs create / webhooks tail — the must-ship reference integration.

rows.title: Agent at the front door
rows.body: OAuth app → SDK → /api/v1, with audit-log rows as the proof.

slides.footer: Depth over breadth. Proof over promises. The TTFE drill is the rubric.
